family_context.preferred_cover_geography: Georgia and Germany, ideally worldwide excluding the US

method_notes: This artifact documents public account-access friction observed at the first reachable plan-detail, quote, or application entry point for each staged candidate.

The purpose is discovery-only: record whether public plan details are accessible, whether quote entry appears public, and whether signup, account creation, login walls, contact capture, or other gating is visible before meaningful quote/detail access.

A visible anti-bot challenge or technical wall is recorded as observed friction even when it may not be a customer identity requirement, because it still blocks access to quote contents in a reproducible public scrape.
Uncertainty is preserved whenever the run did not traverse far enough to determine downstream verification or binding-purchase requirements.

records.plan_name: International Health Insurance / Global Medical Cover
evidence_ledger_entries: Quote page says “Get a quote in just 2 minutes” and immediately asks “Where will you be living for the duration of the policy?” on a public country selector page.
The page separately offers “Continue previous quote,” implying quote retrieval may exist later, but the initial quote entry itself is public.
uncertainty_notes: This run did not progress far enough to determine whether later quote retrieval, save-and-return, or purchase steps require account verification.

evidence_ledger_entries: The captured quote URL returned a page titled “Challenge Validation” instead of quote fields.
The extracted content only exposed challenge/iframe fragments rather than quote details, so the quote path is effectively gated at entry in this run.
uncertainty_notes: The public challenge may be anti-bot rather than a customer login requirement; account-creation or email/phone verification requirements beyond the challenge remain unverified.

records.plan_name: Private Health Insurance & Medical Insurance for Individuals and Families
evidence_ledger_entries: The official page publicly exposes a country selector with Georgia and Germany visible and a “See plans” action.
No signup, login, email capture, or phone verification requirement was visible before reaching the country-selection step.
uncertainty_notes: This run did not verify whether later premium output or application steps introduce account or contact-verification requirements.

evidence_ledger_entries: AXA’s public quote page asks “What length of cover are you looking for?” and offers online quote links plus callback and phone options.
The first visible decision flow is public and did not require an account before the user could reach quote-routing choices.
uncertainty_notes: The downstream quote.axaglobalhealthcare.com flow was not fully traversed here, so later verification requirements remain unverified.

evidence_ledger_entries: APRIL states “International health insurance for everyone, 100% online” and presents a public “Find a plan / Get a quote” selector on the landing page.
The page is navigable without signup and lets users choose cover type, country, and language before any observed account gate.
uncertainty_notes: The run did not confirm whether APRIL later requires email verification or account creation before showing final prices or binding coverage.

evidence_ledger_entries: William Russell says users can “Use the online quote tool” and that prices are shown within minutes.
The public plan page exposes the quote link and pricing examples without requiring login or registration to read plan details.
uncertainty_notes: The dedicated quote app was referenced but not deeply traversed here, so later account, email, or phone verification remains unverified.

records.plan_name: First'Expat+ / International Health Insurance for Individuals
evidence_ledger_entries: MSH’s page is publicly accessible and starts with “Tell us about yourself. You are” plus profile choices such as individual, company, broker, and NGO.
The page also exposes “Already insured with MSH, I log in,” showing that member login exists, but it is not required for the initial profile-selection step.
uncertainty_notes: This run did not advance through the individual flow, so any later contact capture, account creation, or verification requirements remain unverified.

records.plan_name: SimpleCare / WorldCare International Health Insurance Plans
evidence_ledger_entries: Now Health’s plan-comparison page is publicly readable and shows product families, benefit tables, and document-library references.
No quote, signup, login wall, or verification requirement was observed before accessing the plan comparison itself.
uncertainty_notes: A distinct public quote-entry path was not verified in this run, so quote-stage friction remains unknown.

evidence_ledger_entries: IMG publicly shows plan cards with “Buy Now” and “See Price” links for Global Medical plans.
The marketing page exposes those actions and product details without requiring an account at the landing-page stage.
uncertainty_notes: This run did not open the downstream purchase/price pages, so later signup or identity verification requirements remain unverified.

evidence_ledger_entries: The Aetna page is structured around members, brokers, and employers rather than a public individual-family quote form.
For members, the visible action is “Log in to your member website,” and no public direct retail individual quote path was verified on the captured page.
uncertainty_notes: Because this page appears employer/member oriented, it is unclear whether a separate retail individual/family quote flow exists elsewhere or what verification it would require.

records.plan_name: Expat Health Insurance / International Health Insurance comparison
evidence_ledger_entries: Foyer publicly exposes “Receive a quote,” “Get a quick quote,” and “Apply now” from the plan-comparison page.
Plan details, brochure download, and quote-entry CTAs were all visible before any observed login or account gate.
uncertainty_notes: The quick-quote/application flow itself was not fully traversed here, so later account or verification requirements remain unverified.

evidence_ledger_entries: Henner’s individuals-and-families page is publicly readable and explains the offering without requiring login.
This run did not capture a distinct quote/application path, so friction at the quote stage could not be observed.
uncertainty_notes: Because no quote flow was verified, signup, account creation, email capture, or phone verification requirements are unknown.

records.plan_name: YouGenio individual international health insurance plans
evidence_ledger_entries: The application starts publicly with “Get a quotation in seconds. Get your health insurance in minutes.” and shows steps from Quote through Confirmation.
To proceed without consultation, the applicant must tick a waiver: “You need to consent before you can proceed.”
uncertainty_notes: The observed consent requirement is not an account gate, but later identity verification, email confirmation, or portal creation requirements were not verified.

records.plan_name: Evolution Health / Flexible Choices individual international healthcare plans
evidence_ledger_entries: Morgan Price says it is “quick and easy to get an instant quote” via the quick-quote form.

The form asks for residence, currency, age, nationality, and dependents; when online quoting cannot proceed it says “Please contact us by email for a quotation,” and the fallback form asks for Email, Name, Age, and Country of Residence with optional phone callback.
uncertainty_notes: The page clearly captures email for manual follow-up in some cases, but this run did not confirm whether email is mandatory for all quote outputs or only fallback scenarios.

evidence_ledger_entries: PassportCard’s official page publicly shows plan tiers, benefit highlights, and example monthly EUR prices.
No forced signup, account creation, or login wall was observed before viewing plan details and the benefits PDF link.
uncertainty_notes: A direct quote/application path was not verified in this run, so quote-stage contact or verification friction remains unknown.

records.plan_name: Absolute VIP / Universal VIP / Special VIP / Access VIP product family
evidence_ledger_entries: VUMI’s homepage publicly lists health plans and regional selection paths without requiring login to read plan positioning.
The same page separately advertises MyVUMI as a “secured platform” for insureds and an agent portal, indicating account-gated servicing exists, but not at the public discovery stage.
uncertainty_notes: A direct public quote path for individuals/families was not verified here, so pre-quote account or verification friction remains unknown.

evidence_ledger_entries: The staged public evidence is a broker page, not an official insurer quote flow.
The visible quote action is broker-led (“Get a Quote” on InternationalInsurance.com), so direct insurer self-serve quote access was not verified.
uncertainty_notes: The broker’s own quote form friction and any eventual insurer-side account requirements were not fully traced in this run.

records.plan_name: My Global Health individual plans: Blue / Azure / Cobalt / Admiral / Royal
evidence_ledger_entries: MediHelp’s individual-plans page is publicly accessible and shows both “Get a quote” and “BUY ONLINE.”
The BUY ONLINE action points to a “#/login” path, indicating a login-gated online-buy route exists, even though public plan details and a quote entry link are visible without login.
uncertainty_notes: This run did not inspect the separate Get a quote form, so any email/phone verification there remains unverified.

records.plan_name: Global Prima Medical Insurance / Flying Colours legacy entrypoints
evidence_ledger_entries: ALC’s site remains publicly viewable and includes a MyALC claims/member account link.

A notice states: “As of 1 August 2025, new business quotes and sales ... are now handled on our updated website: www.imglobal.com/intl,” so new-business discovery is redirected away from the legacy brand.
uncertainty_notes: This run did not inspect the new IMG-branded destination from the redirect notice for any additional friction.

records.insurer_name: Blue Cross Blue Shield Global Solutions (formerly GeoBlue Xplorer)
records.plan_name: Worldwide Premier / Outside U.S. / Outside U.S. Select
evidence_ledger_entries: The captured discovery evidence is a broker page summarizing BCBS Global Solutions / former GeoBlue Xplorer.
Direct insurer self-serve quote access was not verified from the staged evidence, so discovery currently depends on a broker/intermediary path.
uncertainty_notes: Broker form friction and any subsequent insurer verification requirements were not traced in this run.

evidence_ledger_entries: The captured entry page is a signup screen headed “Your global coverage starts here.”
Before progressing, the user must either “Sign in with Google” or enter Email and Password to “Continue with email,” and the page also asks “Already have an account? Log in.”
uncertainty_notes: The run did not determine whether any anonymous quote-only path exists elsewhere outside this signup URL.

evidence_ledger_entries: Genki’s consultation starts publicly with “Answer a few quick questions” and says users can “See the price and sign up in minutes.”
The first visible flow collects travel and destination answers without showing a login or account-creation gate at entry.
uncertainty_notes: The wording implies signup later in the process, but this run did not verify whether account creation is required before the final price display or only at purchase.

evidence_ledger_entries: ACS invites users to “Request your free, no-obligation expat health insurance quote” through a multi-step request flow.

The final step says “Please provide your contact details below. One of our advisors will get back to you promptly with a personalised proposal,” and the form requests name, date of birth, nationality, and contact details.
uncertainty_notes: The extract did not definitively show which contact fields are mandatory beyond the visible personal-data fields, but the quote path clearly shifts to advisor follow-up rather than instant anonymous pricing.

records.plan_name: Health Protect / Protector Plans / Nimbl Health
evidence_ledger_entries: The captured Outgrow quote URL did not expose quote questions; it returned an “Internet is unstable. Please check your connection” message.
Because the public tool did not render usable quote content in this run, discovery could not verify whether later signup or verification would be required.
uncertainty_notes: The observed failure may be transient or tool-specific rather than a customer-facing hard gate.

records.plan_name: International Health Insurance / Select / Primary+ / Primary+ Lite / Primary
evidence_ledger_entries: The quick-quote form collects people to insure, area of cover, and dates of birth, then says “Please enter your email address below to receive a copy of your quotation.”
Email is part of stage 1 before pressing “PROCEED,” so contact capture occurs before the quote flow advances.
uncertainty_notes: This run did not verify whether the system displays pricing before or only after email submission, but email capture is clearly embedded in the initial quote stage.

evidence_ledger_entries: WellAway Step 1 requires Applicant’s Email, Origin Phone, Destination Phone, nationality, DOB, and other basics before moving on.
The page also states “We keep your information safe! Do you consent with your personal data being processed as described in the Privacy Policy? Click Yes to continue.”
uncertainty_notes: The flow was only inspected at Step 1, so any later account creation or email/phone verification mechanism remains unverified.

evidence_ledger_entries: Securus’s page publicly exposes marketing copy and brochure PDFs without requiring login.
No forced signup, account gate, or contact-verification requirement was observed before accessing the public benefit materials.
uncertainty_notes: A direct public quote or application path was not verified, so quote-stage friction remains unknown.
